claims.source_quote: Fertilizer is the most important and expensive agricultural input. Its contribution in increase in crop yield is from 30 to 50 percent.
claims.claim: Fertilizer is presented as a critical agricultural input that materially contributes to crop yield increases.
claims.effect: increase in crop yield from fertilizer use

claims.source_quote: Almost all of our soils are deficient in nitrogen, 90 percent soils have deficiency of phosphorus while 40 percent are deficient in potash.
claims.claim: Pakistan's soils face widespread nutrient deficiencies, especially nitrogen, phosphorus, and potash deficiencies.
claims.constraint_or_risk: soil deficiencies in nitrogen, phosphorus and potash

claims.source_quote: The domestic production of fertilizers during 2016-17 (July-March) decreased slightly by 0.3 percent over the same period of last fiscal year.
claims.claim: Domestic fertilizer production and imported fertilizer both declined during July-March 2016-17, reducing total fertilizer availability marginally.
claims.effect: total fertilizer availability decreased marginally

comparison.baseline: same period of last fiscal year

claims.source_quote: Total offtake of fertilizer nutrients witnessed increase by 30.5 percent.
claims.claim: Fertilizer nutrient offtake increased sharply in July-March 2016-17, with nitrogen, phosphate, and potash offtake all rising.

comparison.baseline: same period of last fiscal year

claims.source_quote: Increase in offtake of fertilizer is due to substantial reduction in prices of all fertilizer products as a result of subsidy announcement by the government in June,2016
claims.claim: The increase in fertilizer offtake is attributed to lower fertilizer prices following the government's June 2016 subsidy announcement.
claims.policy_or_programme: fertilizer subsidy announcement by the government in June 2016
policy_or_programmes: fertilizer subsidy announcement by the government in June 2016
Government of Punjab SOP and MOP subsidy from 1 February 2017
claims.cause: substantial reduction in prices of all fertilizer products as a result of subsidy announcement
claims.effect: increase in fertilizer offtake

claims.source_quote: Better market prices of agriculture produce especially rice, cotton and sugarcane also had positive effect on fertilizer use.
claims.claim: Better market prices for rice, cotton, and sugarcane positively affected fertilizer use.
claims.cause: better market prices of agriculture produce especially rice, cotton and sugarcane
claims.effect: positive effect on fertilizer use

claims.source_quote: Urea offtake was about 2704 thousand tonnes, leaving inventory of 1565 thousand tonnes for Rabi 2016-17.
claims.claim: Kharif 2016 urea and DAP supply left carryover inventories for the upcoming Rabi 2016-17 season after offtake.
claims.effect: closing inventories for Rabi 2016-17

claims.source_quote: Urea offtake during current Rabi 2016-17 was around 3085 thousand tonnes, against 4405 thousand tonnes of total availability
claims.claim: Rabi 2016-17 urea availability exceeded offtake, leaving a closing balance for the next season.
claims.effect: closing balance of urea for next season

claims.source_quote: Total availability of urea during Kharif 2017 will be about 4018 thousand tonnes
claims.claim: Kharif 2017 urea availability is expected to cover expected offtake and leave a closing balance.
claims.effect: expected closing balance of urea

claims.source_quote: there is gap of 261 thousand tonnes of DAP which should be met through imports by private sector.
claims.claim: Kharif 2017 DAP availability is expected to fall short of expected offtake, requiring private-sector imports to fill the gap.
claims.constraint_or_risk: gap of 261 thousand tonnes of DAP
claims.cause: total availability of DAP will be 639 thousand tonnes against expected offtake of 900 thousand tonnes
claims.effect: gap should be met through imports by private sector

claims.source_quote: Federal Seed Certification & Registration Department (FSC&RD) is an attached department of Ministry of National Food Security & Research
claims.claim: FSC&RD is mandated to regulate seed quality for various crops under the Ministry of National Food Security & Research.
actors: Ministry of National Food Security & Research

claims.source_quote: Seed quality being a key input in crop production plays a vital role for sustainable agricultural production and national food security.
claims.claim: Seed quality is described as a key input for sustainable agricultural production and national food security.
claims.effect: supports sustainable agricultural production and national food security

claims.source_quote: the registration of Seed Companies and other related issues were withheld till notification of Seed (Business Regulation) Rules, 2016.
claims.claim: Registration of seed companies and related issues was withheld until notification of the Seed (Business Regulation) Rules, 2016.
claims.cause: pending notification of Seed (Business Regulation) Rules, 2016
claims.effect: registration of seed companies and related issues were withheld

claims.source_quote: The Gazette Notification of Seed (Business Regulation) Rules, 2016 took place on 28[th] September, 2016.
claims.claim: The Seed (Business Regulation) Rules, 2016 were notified in the Gazette on 28 September 2016.

claims.source_quote: The Registration to do seed business comes under Section 22B with prescribed amount of fee
claims.claim: Seed business registration was placed under Section 22B with prescribed certification and registration fees expected to generate government revenue.
claims.cause: prescribed fee for Seed Certification and Registration Services
claims.effect: generate a sustainable amount to the government exchequer

claims.source_quote: A total of 199819.54 acres of different crops ... were inspected for certification purposes.
claims.claim: Public and private seed agency crop areas were inspected for certification purposes across multiple crop types.
claims.indicator_or_subject: field crop inspection for seed certification

claims.source_quote: A total quantity of  428112  m.tons locally produced seed of major and minor crops was sampled and tested
claims.claim: Locally produced seeds of major and minor crops were sampled and tested for purity, germination, and seed health.

claims.source_quote: 91 cases were filed in different Courts of Law against the seed dealers found selling substandard seeds
claims.claim: Seed Act enforcement actions were taken against dealers selling substandard seeds, including court cases and seed seizures.
claims.cause: seed dealers found selling substandard seeds
claims.effect: cases filed in courts and seeds seized

claims.source_quote: imported seed of various crops/hybrids ... were tested under Seed (Truth in Labeling) Rules, 1991
claims.claim: Imported seed consignments of several crops and hybrids were tested at Lahore and Karachi ports under Seed (Truth in Labeling) Rules, 1991.

claims.source_quote: One thousand and thirty seven (1037) seed samples of different crops ... were tested for seed purity, seed moisture and other components.
claims.claim: The Central Seed Testing Laboratory tested seed samples from FSC&RD field stations for purity, moisture, and other components.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Central Seed Testing Laboratory seed testing

claims.source_quote: CSTL applied to ISTA through electronic communication for getting Accreditation status.
claims.claim: CSTL pursued ISTA accreditation, submitted quality documents, qualified pre-evaluation, and expected final evaluation by an ISTA audit team in April 2017.
claims.effect: getting accreditation status

claims.source_quote: Seed Health Testing Laboratory, FSC&RD has made headway towards laboratory accreditation by starting Inter Laboratory Comparison (ILC).
claims.claim: FSC&RD's Seed Health Testing Laboratory advanced toward accreditation by beginning inter-laboratory comparison work with a Japanese laboratory.
actors: Department of DUS Testing and Seed Inspection
Centre for Seeds and Seedlings, NARO (NCSS), Japan
claims.effect: headway towards laboratory accreditation

claims.source_quote: Pakistan being a member of World Trade Organization (WTO) is obliged to provide rights to the breeders of new plant varieties
claims.claim: Pakistan's WTO membership obliged it to provide rights to breeders of new plant varieties under TRIPs Article 27.3(b).
policy_or_programmes: Trade Related Aspects for Intellectual Property Rights (TRIP’s) Agreement
claims.constraint_or_risk: obligation to provide rights to breeders of new plant varieties

claims.source_quote: The Plant Breeders’ Rights Bill, 2016 after being approved by both the houses of the Parliament, was signed by the President
claims.claim: The Plant Breeders’ Rights Bill, 2016 passed through both houses of Parliament and was signed by the President on 5 December 2016.
claims.actor: President of Islamic Republic of Pakistan
actors: Senate Standing Committee on National Food Security and Research
President of Islamic Republic of Pakistan

claims.subsection: b) Importance of the Plant Breeders’ Rights Act, 2016
claims.source_quote: only the public sector was given the dominant role in seed development and registration processes
claims.claim: Under the Seed Act, 1976, seed development and registration were dominated by the public sector with no assigned private-sector role.
claims.indicator_or_subject: private sector role in seed development and registration
claims.constraint_or_risk: no role assigned to the private sector
claims.cause: Seed Act, 1976 gave the public sector the dominant role

claims.subsection: b) Importance of the Plant Breeders’ Rights Act, 2016
claims.source_quote: The promulgation of PBR law in the country will not only complement the recently passed Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015
claims.claim: The PBR law was expected to complement the Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 and support research-based innovation and technology-led seed industry development.
claims.effect: usher in research based innovations and technology led developments

claims.subsection: b) Importance of the Plant Breeders’ Rights Act, 2016
claims.source_quote: Many foreign companies are reluctant to invest in our seed sector due to lack of protection for their products
claims.claim: Foreign companies were reluctant to invest in Pakistan's seed sector because product protection was lacking under the previous variety protection system.
claims.constraint_or_risk: lack of protection for products and absence of an effective variety protection system
claims.cause: lack of protection for their products because of absence of an effective variety protection system
claims.effect: foreign companies are reluctant to invest in the seed sector

claims.source_quote: hybrid plant varieties and other emerging technologies.
claims.claim: After the PBR law's promulgation, the seed industry was expected to see greater investment and technology transfer for hybrid plant varieties and emerging technologies.
claims.indicator_or_subject: seed industry investment and technology transfer
claims.effect: quantum jump in investment and transfer of modern technologies

claims.subsection: c) Formulation of Plant Breeders’ Rights Rules, 2017
claims.source_quote: has finalized Draft Rules under the PBR Act, 2016,which have been circulated to concerned stakeholders
claims.claim: An inter-ministerial and inter-departmental committee finalized draft rules under the PBR Act, 2016 and circulated them to stakeholders for comments.
claims.actor: Ministry of National Food Security and Research
actors: Ministry of National Food Security and Research
claims.effect: stakeholders asked for viewpoints

claims.subsection: d) Passage and Implementation of Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015
claims.source_quote: Seed Rules, 2016 have • been approved and circulated. Implementation • of activities ... are being expedited.
claims.claim: After passage of the Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015, Seed Rules, 2016 were approved and circulated, and implementation activities were being expedited.
claims.cause: passage of Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 by Parliament
claims.effect: implementation activities are being expedited

claims.subsection: e) Impact of Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 Rules
claims.source_quote: Private sector has been given larger role in all areas of seed testing, production, multiplication and processing;
claims.claim: The Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 Rules expanded the private sector's role across seed testing, production, multiplication, and processing.
claims.indicator_or_subject: private sector role in seed sector
claims.effect: larger role for private sector in seed testing, production, multiplication and processing

claims.subsection: e) Impact of Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 Rules
claims.source_quote: Accredited seed testing laboratories allowed to be established by public and private seed sectors
claims.claim: Public and private seed sectors were allowed to establish accredited seed testing laboratories to improve quality assurance and connect with seed markets.
claims.effect: enhance internal seed quality assurance and link with national and international seed markets for export

claims.subsection: e) Impact of Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 Rules
claims.source_quote: Effective deterrence is expected to be created by enhancement of penalties against sale of fake and substandard seed;
claims.claim: Enhanced penalties against fake and substandard seed sales were expected to create effective deterrence.
claims.indicator_or_subject: penalties for fake and substandard seed
claims.constraint_or_risk: sale of fake and substandard seed
claims.effect: effective deterrence

claims.subsection: e) Impact of Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 Rules
claims.source_quote: mandatory registration of seed business has been introduced.
claims.claim: Mandatory seed business registration was introduced to bring discipline to the seed industry.
claims.cause: to bring discipline in the seed industry
claims.effect: mandatory registration of seed business introduced

claims.source_quote: On receiving seed samples, crop variety registration trials are carried out for two years at suitable locations
claims.claim: FSC&RD conducts two-year crop variety registration trials at suitable locations to determine DUS characteristics of candidate varieties.
claims.effect: determine DUS characteristics of the variety

claims.source_quote: A total of about 57 new candidate lines have been studied for DUS trials.
claims.claim: About 57 new candidate lines were studied for DUS trials.
claims.indicator_or_subject: new candidate lines for DUS trials

claims.source_quote: FSC&RD has registered one new fruit plant nursery established at Quetta
claims.claim: FSC&RD registered a new fruit plant nursery at Quetta to promote disease-free and true-to-type nursery fruit plants.
claims.cause: to promote production and dissemination of disease free and true-to-type nursery fruit plants
claims.effect: registered one new fruit plant nursery established at Quetta

claims.source_quote: Registration cases of ten fruit plant nurseries are in-process;
claims.claim: Registration cases for ten fruit plant nurseries were in process.

claims.source_quote: Minimum Fruit Plant Certification Standards for citrus, mango and guava have been drafted and submitted to National Seed Council for approval;
claims.claim: Minimum Fruit Plant Certification Standards for citrus, mango, and guava were drafted and submitted to the National Seed Council for approval, while olive standards were ready for submission.
policy_or_programmes: Minimum Fruit Plant Certification Standards for citrus, mango and guava
Minimum Fruit Plant Certification Standards for olive
claims.effect: submitted or ready for submission for approval

claims.source_quote: Following two developmental projects have been drafted by FSC&RD and submitted to Ministry of National Food Security & Research for approval
claims.claim: FSC&RD drafted and submitted two development projects for approval: upgrading and accrediting the Karachi seed testing laboratory and upgrading seed certification services in Balochistan.
actors: Ministry of National Food Security & Research
policy_or_programmes: Up-gradation and Accreditation of Seed Testing Laboratory, FSC&RD, Karachi
Up-gradation of seed certification services in Balochistan
claims.effect: submitted to Ministry of National Food Security & Research for approval

claims.source_quote: FSC&RD International Cooperation section was in the process of deliberations during the period FY 2016-17 (July-March)
claims.claim: FSC&RD's International Cooperation section was deliberating cooperation proposals for seed sector development during July-March FY2016-17.
claims.indicator_or_subject: international cooperation for seed sector development

claims.source_quote: different cooperation proposals with the following countries and international organizations; ECO, Jordan, Senegal, Uzbekistan
claims.claim: FSC&RD cooperation proposals involved multiple countries and international organizations, including ECO, FAO, SAARC, D-8, China, Japan, Turkey, Germany, and others.
claims.geography: Pakistan; ECO; Jordan; Senegal; Uzbekistan; Turkmenistan; Middle East; Germany; Bahrain; Qatar; Italy; Tajikistan; Japan; Thailand; Turkey; China; Brazil; Papua New Guinea

claims.source_quote: a total number of 37,634 tractors were locally manufactured compared to the production of 21,229 during same period last year
claims.claim: Local tractor manufacturing increased significantly during July-March 2016-17 compared with the same period last year.

claims.source_quote: as the GST on locally manufactured/imported  tractors have been reduced from 10 percent to 5 percent that has increased the demand of tractors.
claims.claim: The reduction in GST on locally manufactured and imported tractors from 10 percent to 5 percent increased tractor demand.
claims.policy_or_programme: GST reduction on locally manufactured/imported tractors
policy_or_programmes: GST reduction on locally manufactured/imported tractors
claims.cause: GST on locally manufactured/imported tractors reduced from 10 percent to 5 percent
claims.effect: increased demand of tractors

claims.source_quote: the import of agriculture machinery has witnessed a significant growth of 25.6 percent during FY 2016-17(July-March)
claims.claim: Agricultural machinery imports grew significantly during July-March FY2016-17 compared with the corresponding period last year.

claims.source_quote: customs duty on import of harvesters and planters was reduced to zero to promote mechanized harvesting and planting operations
claims.claim: Customs duty on harvester and planter imports was reduced to zero to promote mechanized harvesting and planting operations.
claims.indicator_or_subject: customs duty on harvesters and planters
claims.policy_or_programme: customs duty reduction on harvesters and planters
policy_or_programmes: customs duty reduction on harvesters and planters
